_note: Grounded 'what the city funded' lines for the SF timeline's What-changed section. RULE identical to sf_timeline_archive.json: every figure is transcribed VERBATIM from a scanned SF serial financial report on the Internet Archive, with an exact djvu substring `quote`, the printed `digitrun`, and the scan `leaf` for the deep link. export_sf_timeline.py re-verifies each quote + digit-run against the live OCR and drops any line that no longer grounds. These are NOT exhaustive rankings — the 1888 lines are illustrative ledger entries (schools/police/interest were larger and are not machine-cleanly extractable from the 19th-c. compendium OCR); the 1913 lines are department totals as printed (the 1912-13 column). `framing` records exactly which. Deeper per-era extraction (1895/1968/1986) is a follow-up. The modern composition is computed live from the budget marts by the exporter, not seeded here.

archive.framing: illustrative lines from the ledger (verbatim), not a ranking
